title: 子供たちと伝統文化①（意見）
question: Should children be made to learn about their country's traditional culture?
question_jp: 子供は自国の伝統文化を学ばされるべきだと思いますか？
model_answer: I don't think so, because some children just aren't really into traditional culture.
For example, I grew out of festivals when I was a teenager, though the memories still stick in my memory.
Forcing them can hold them back from ever enjoying it, in my opinion.
Instead, I reckon it's better to enlighten kids about other cultures so they become well-rounded.

items.meaning: 〜に夢中である・大ファンである
成長して〜から卒業する

items.collocation: hold them back from enjoying it

items.meaning: 多才な・幅広い教養のある

drills.jp: 一部の子供はそもそも伝統文化に夢中ではないと思う。
drills.model_en: Some children just aren't really into traditional culture.

drills.jp: 10代で祭りから卒業したが、思い出は今も記憶に焼きついている。
drills.model_en: I grew out of festivals as a teenager, but they still stick in my memory.

drills.jp: その代わり、子供に他の文化を教えて多才にする方が良いと思う。
drills.model_en: Instead, it's better to enlighten kids so they become well-rounded.
